```json
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Classic TOCTOU race condition will oversell inventory under concurrent requests"
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "Race condition: stock check and decrement are not atomic",
      "risk": "Two concurrent requests both read stock=1, both pass the check, both decrement — resulting in stock going negative and overselling inventory. This is a textbook Time-of-Check-to-Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) bug.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart function, lines 5-9 (getStock → check → addItem → decrementStock)",
      "reproduction": "1. Product X has stock=1. 2. User A calls addToCart(A, X, 1) — reads stock=1, passes check, yields at await addItem. 3. User B calls addToCart(B, X, 1) — reads stock=1, passes check. 4. Both proceed: stock decremented twice → stock = -1, two users believe they purchased the last item.",
      "fix": "Use an atomic conditional update: `UPDATE products SET stock = stock - $quantity WHERE id = $productId AND stock >= $quantity`. Check affected rows — if 0 rows updated, throw insufficient stock. This eliminates the read-then-write race entirely. Alternatively, wrap the entire operation in a serializable transaction or use SELECT FOR UPDATE.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Inventory accuracy; users must not be able to purchase more than available stock"
    },
    {
      "title": "Non-atomic cart add + stock decrement causes inconsistent state on partial failure",
      "risk": "If addItem succeeds but decrementStock fails (DB timeout, connection drop, constraint violation), the cart contains an item but stock was never reduced. Conversely, if the process crashes between the two calls, state is permanently inconsistent with no recovery path.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart function, lines 8-9 (addItem then decrementStock as separate operations)",
      "reproduction": "1. addItem succeeds — cart now has product. 2. decrementStock throws (e.g., network timeout). 3. Function throws, but cart item persists. 4. Stock is never decremented — inventory drift accumulates. 5. At checkout, user has a ghost item or stock is wrong.",
      "fix": "Wrap both operations in a database transaction: `await db.transaction(async (tx) => { ... })`. Both succeed or both roll back. The atomic stock decrement from Finding 1 should also live inside this transaction.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Data consistency between cart state and inventory levels"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on quantity allows zero, negative, or non-integer values",
      "risk": "A public API caller can pass quantity=0 (no-op that pollutes cart), quantity=-5 (increments stock via decrement, creates free inventory), or quantity=1.5 (fractional stock corruption). Since this is called from a public API, all inputs must be treated as hostile.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "addToCart function, quantity parameter — no validation before use",
      "reproduction": "1. Call addToCart(user, product, -10). 2. stock check: stock (say 5) < -10 is false → passes. 3. addItem adds -10 quantity to cart. 4. decrementStock decrements by -10 → stock increases by 10. 5. Attacker has manufactured inventory out of nothing.",
      "fix": "Add validation at function entry: `if (!Number.isInteger(quantity) || quantity < 1) throw new Error('Invalid quantity');`. Also validate userId and productId are non-empty strings.",
      "time_to_fix": "10 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Input integrity; API security against parameter manipulation"
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No upper bound on quantity",
      "description": "Even with positive integer validation, a user could request quantity=999999. Should enforce a per-request maximum and/or per-user cart limit to prevent stock hoarding and abuse.",
      "location": "addToCart function, quantity parameter"
    },
    {
      "title": "No idempotency protection",
      "description": "If a client retries a failed/timed-out request, the same item gets added twice. A public API handling cart mutations should accept an idempotency key to make retries safe.",
      "location": "addToCart function signature"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Consider using an optimistic concurrency pattern with a version column on the products table as an alternative to SELECT FOR UPDATE, to reduce lock contention on hot products",
    "Add structured logging for stock-related operations to enable inventory audit trails and drift detection"
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "Every concurrent request to popular products is a potential oversell. Risk scales linearly with traffic — flash sales or viral products will trigger this immediately.",
    "rollback_capability": "N/A — this is a logic review, not a deployment diff. Fix can be deployed as a standard release.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Fix is backward compatible — same function signature, same behavior for non-concurrent happy path.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No monitoring mentioned. Should alert on negative stock values and cart/stock count mismatches.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": false,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": []
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Do not ship this code to production. Fix the race condition and atomicity issues first."
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 97,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "TOCTOU race condition — this is deterministic under concurrency, not speculative",
      "Partial failure inconsistency — two non-transactional writes with no rollback",
      "Negative quantity exploit — trivially reproducible via any HTTP client"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [],
    "assumptions": [
      "db.products.decrementStock performs a simple subtraction without its own atomic guard",
      "db.cart.addItem and db.products.decrementStock are not already wrapped in an external transaction by the caller"
    ]
  }
}
```

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this code for production risks.

Key hypotheses:
1. Race condition between stock check and decrement - TOCTOU
2. No input validation on quantity
3. No atomicity - cart add succeeds but stock decrement could fail

Let me classify: These are all code-level fixes (transaction, validation, atomic operations) - not architectural concerns. Classification: `none`.
